concept:
Tested 3 ? A non tender abdominal mass associated with elevated VMA and HVA is: Neuroblastoma
What is the most common type of tracheoesophageal fistula (TEF)? What is the diagnosis?
Type: Esophageal atresia with distal TEF (85%)
Diagnosed by: Unable to pass NG tube
What medical conditions are not contraindications to vaccination?
Mild illness and/or low grade fever
Current antibiotic therapy
Prematurity
What are the tests to rule out shaken baby syndrome?
Opthalmologic exam
CT
MRI
A neonate has meconium ileus indicating: Cystic fibrosis or Hirschsprung's disease
Bilious emesis within hours after the first feeding indicates: Duodenal atresia
A two month old presents with nonbilious, projectile emesis. What are the first two steps in management?
Correct metabolic abnormalities
Correct pyloric stenosis with pyloromyotomy
What is the most common primary immunodeficiency? Selective IgA deficiency
An infant has a high fever and onset of rash as the fever breaks. What is he at risk for? Febrile seizures (roseola infantum)
Patient:
is a boy
has chronic respiratory infections
whose Nitroblue tetrazolium test was positive What immunodeficiency is indicated: Chronic granulomatous disease
Patient:
is a child
with eczema
with thrombocytopenia
with high levels of IgA What immunodeficiency is indicated? Wiskott-Aldrich syndrome
What immunodeficiency Patient:
is a four month old
is male
has life threatening Pseudomonas infection indicating: Bruton's X-linked agammaglobulinemia
What is the acute phase treatment for Kawasaki disease?
High dose aspirin for inflammation and fever
IVIG to prevent coronary artery aneurysms
Patient:
is one year old
is able to cruise
can use two-finger pincer grasp
can babble
can imitate actions Diagnosis? Suspect language delay
What is the treatment for mild and severe unconjugated hyperbilirubinemia?
Mild:Phototherapy
Severe:Exchange transfusion
A patient
with a sudden onset of mental status changes
with emesis
with liver dysfunction
who recently took asprin indicating: Reye's syndrome
A child has loss of red light reflex indicating: Suspect retinoblastoma
What are the vaccinations for a healthy six month-old?
HBV
DTap
Hib
IPV
PCV
What is Tanner stage III in a six year-old female? Precocious puberty
What is an infection of the small airways with epidemics in winter and spring? RSV bronchiolitis
What is the cause of neonatal RDS? Surfactant deficiency
What is the condition associated with red "currant-jelly" stools? Intussusception
What congenital heart disease causes secondary hypertension (HTN)? Coarctation of the aorta
What is the first-line treatment for otitis media? Amoxicillin x 10 days
What is the most common pathogen that causes croup? PIV type 1
A homeless child is small for his age and has peeling skin and a swollen belly indicating: Kwashiorkor (protein malnutrition)
What syndrome comes from an X-linked defect causing:
mental retardation
gout
self-mutilation
choreoathetosis?
Lesch-Nyhan syndrome (purine salvage problem with HGPRTase deficiency)
A newborn female has continuous "machinery murmur" indicating: Patent ductus arteriosus (PDA)
